composition-division.description: افتراض أن ما ينطبق على الأجزاء ينطبق على الكل (التركيب)، أو أن ما ينطبق على الكل ينطبق على كل جزء (التقسيم).
composition-division.responseStrategy: ميِّز بين الأجزاء والكل: «ما ينطبق على الأجزاء الفردية لا ينطبق بالضرورة على المجموعة، والعكس صحيح. هل يمكنك إثبات أنه ينطبق على كلا المستويين؟»

examples.text: Every player on this team is a star, so this must be the best team in the league.
examples.explanation: Individual talent doesn't guarantee team chemistry, strategy, or overall performance.

examples.text: Atoms are invisible. Humans are made of atoms. Therefore, humans are invisible.
examples.explanation: Properties of atoms don't necessarily transfer to the larger structures they compose.

examples.text: This university is prestigious, so every course it offers must be excellent.
examples.explanation: Overall institutional reputation doesn't guarantee the quality of each individual course.

examples.text: Hydrogen is not wet. Oxygen is not wet. Therefore, water (H2O) is not wet.
examples.explanation: Properties of individual elements don't necessarily carry over to the compounds they form.

quizScenarios.text: "Each ingredient in this recipe is delicious on its own, so the dish must taste amazing."
quizScenarios.explanation: Individual ingredient quality doesn't guarantee they'll combine well together. This is the composition fallacy.

quizScenarios.text: "The United States is a wealthy country, so every American must be wealthy."
quizScenarios.explanation: National wealth doesn't mean each individual citizen is wealthy. This is the division fallacy.

appeal-to-nature.description: الادعاء بأن شيئاً ما جيد لأنه طبيعي أو سيئ لأنه غير طبيعي. كون الشيء طبيعياً لا يجعله تلقائياً جيداً أو آمناً.
appeal-to-nature.responseStrategy: قدِّم أمثلة مضادة: «كثير من الأشياء الطبيعية ضارة وكثير من الأشياء الاصطناعية مفيدة. هل يمكنك شرح لماذا كونه طبيعياً يجعله أفضل تحديداً؟»

examples.text: Herbal remedies are better than medicine because they're all natural.
examples.explanation: Many effective medicines are synthetic, and many natural substances are toxic. 'Natural' doesn't mean 'better.'

examples.text: Homosexuality is unnatural, so it's wrong.
examples.explanation: Same-sex behavior is observed across many species in nature. But regardless, naturalness doesn't determine morality.

examples.text: We shouldn't use artificial sweeteners because they're not natural.
examples.explanation: Being artificial doesn't automatically make something harmful, and being natural doesn't guarantee safety.

quizScenarios.text: "I only use natural cleaning products because chemicals are bad for you. If it's from nature, it's safe."
quizScenarios.explanation: Many natural substances are toxic (e.g., ricin, cyanide in apple seeds), and 'chemical' simply describes any substance. Natural doesn't equal safe.

quizScenarios.text: "Our ancestors didn't use computers, so screen time must be inherently bad for us."
quizScenarios.explanation: What our ancestors did or didn't do doesn't determine what's good or bad for modern humans.

genetic-fallacy.description: الحكم على شيء بأنه جيد أو سيئ بناءً على مصدره أو أصله، بدلاً من تقييم قيمته الحالية.
genetic-fallacy.responseStrategy: افصل الأصل عن القيمة: «مصدر الفكرة لا يحدد ما إذا كانت صحيحة. لنقيِّم الحجة بناءً على أدلتها الخاصة.»

examples.text: That idea came from a controversial politician, so it must be bad.
examples.explanation: The origin of an idea doesn't determine its quality. Even controversial figures can have good ideas.

examples.text: This tradition has been around for centuries, so it must be valuable.
examples.explanation: Age of a practice doesn't prove its value — many old traditions have been harmful.

examples.text: She grew up in a rough neighborhood, so her opinions on economics can't be sophisticated.
examples.explanation: Someone's background doesn't determine the quality of their reasoning.

quizScenarios.text: "That management technique was developed in Japan, so it won't work in American companies."
quizScenarios.explanation: The country of origin doesn't determine whether a management technique is effective. It should be evaluated on its merits.

quizScenarios.text: "This scientific paper was funded by a pharmaceutical company, so all its conclusions are automatically wrong."
quizScenarios.explanation: While funding sources warrant scrutiny, they don't automatically invalidate research findings.

middle-ground.description: افتراض أن الحقيقة يجب أن تكون حلاً وسطاً بين موقفين متعارضين. أحياناً يكون أحد الجانبين على حق بالكامل.
middle-ground.responseStrategy: أشِر إلى أن الحقيقة ليست دائماً في الوسط: «الحل الوسط ممتاز للمفاوضات، لكن الحقائق لا تُحسم بتقسيم الفارق. ماذا تدعم الأدلة؟»

examples.text: Some scientists say the Earth is 4.5 billion years old and others say 6,000 years. The truth is probably somewhere in between.
examples.explanation: The scientific evidence overwhelmingly supports 4.5 billion years — the middle isn't automatically correct.

examples.text: She says the project will take 2 weeks, he says 2 years. Let's compromise and say 6 months.
examples.explanation: The correct timeline should be based on analysis, not splitting the difference between estimates.

examples.text: One side says vaccines are safe, the other says they're dangerous. The truth must be in the middle.
examples.explanation: Overwhelming evidence supports vaccine safety. Splitting the difference doesn't represent the scientific consensus.

quizScenarios.text: "The prosecution says the defendant committed 10 crimes, and the defense says zero. The truth is probably around 5 crimes."
quizScenarios.explanation: Legal truth isn't found by averaging claims. Evidence must determine guilt for each charge independently.

quizScenarios.text: "Half of experts say this bridge is structurally sound, and half say it will collapse. Let's compromise and allow only light traffic."
quizScenarios.explanation: Bridge safety isn't a matter of compromise. Engineering analysis should determine whether it's safe or not.

texas-sharpshooter.description: انتقاء مجموعات البيانات التي تناسب حجة ما مع تجاهل البيانات التي لا تتوافق. مثل رسم الهدف حول مكان سقوط الرصاصات.
texas-sharpshooter.responseStrategy: اسأل ما إذا كان النمط قد تم التنبؤ به قبل جمع البيانات، أم اكتُشف بعد ذلك. قل: «هل تختار فقط البيانات التي تتوافق مع استنتاجك؟»

examples.text: A psychic predicted 'something bad will happen in a major city' and then points to any negative event as proof.
examples.explanation: The prediction was so vague that almost any event could confirm it — the target was drawn after the shot.

examples.text: This neighborhood has a cancer cluster, so the local power plant must be causing it.
examples.explanation: Random variation can produce clusters. Without controlled study, you can't assume the power plant is the cause.

examples.text: Our product launched on a Tuesday and succeeded. Tuesday launches must be the key to success!
examples.explanation: Picking one successful data point and attributing it to the day of the week ignores all other factors.

quizScenarios.text: "I looked at the last 100 years of stock market data and found a pattern: stocks always rise in months with the letter 'R.' This is my foolproof trading strategy."
quizScenarios.explanation: The pattern was found by searching data after the fact. Random data will always have some apparent patterns that don't predict future results.

quizScenarios.text: "Three of our top salespeople all drink green tea. Green tea must be the secret to sales success!"
quizScenarios.explanation: This cherry-picks a coincidental trait shared by a few people while ignoring what the other top performers do.

burden-of-proof.description: نقل عبء الإثبات إلى الطرف الخاطئ. من يقدم الادعاء هو من يجب عليه تقديم الدليل.
burden-of-proof.responseStrategy: ذكِّرهم بمن يتحمل عبء الإثبات: «أنت من أطلق الادعاء، لذا تقع عليك مسؤولية تقديم الأدلة. لست بحاجة لدحض شيء لم يُثبَت.»

examples.text: I believe aliens built the pyramids. Can you prove they didn't?
examples.explanation: The person claiming alien construction has the burden of providing evidence for their extraordinary claim.

examples.text: This supplement cures all diseases until someone proves it doesn't.
examples.explanation: Medical claims require evidence from the claimant, not disproof from skeptics.

examples.text: My conspiracy theory is true unless you can debunk every single detail.
examples.explanation: The conspiracy theorist needs to prove their theory, not demand others disprove it point by point.

quizScenarios.text: "My new energy drink improves IQ by 30 points. Show me a study that says it doesn't!"
quizScenarios.explanation: The maker of this claim must provide evidence that the drink improves IQ, not demand that others disprove it.

quizScenarios.text: "I sense a ghost in this room. Unless you can prove there's no ghost here, my feeling is valid evidence."
quizScenarios.explanation: The person claiming a ghost exists needs to provide evidence, not shift the burden to skeptics.

anecdotal.description: استخدام التجربة الشخصية بدلاً من الأدلة المنهجية. قصة واحدة لا تثبت نمطاً عاماً.
anecdotal.responseStrategy: اعترف بالقصة، ثم وسِّع النطاق: «هذه حالة مثيرة للاهتمام، لكن ماذا تقول البيانات الإجمالية؟ مثال واحد لا يمثل الصورة الكاملة.»

examples.text: My grandfather smoked his whole life and lived to 95, so smoking can't be that bad.
examples.explanation: One person's experience doesn't change the statistical reality that smoking significantly increases health risks.

examples.text: I never wear a seatbelt and I've never been hurt, so seatbelts are unnecessary.
examples.explanation: Personal luck doesn't negate decades of data showing seatbelts save lives.

examples.text: My friend dropped out of college and became a millionaire, so college is a waste of time.
examples.explanation: One success story doesn't invalidate the statistical advantages of higher education.

quizScenarios.text: "I know someone who ate fast food every day and was perfectly healthy. So all these studies about unhealthy diets must be wrong."
quizScenarios.explanation: One person's experience doesn't invalidate large-scale nutritional research based on thousands of subjects.

quizScenarios.text: "My neighbor didn't vaccinate her kids and they're fine. Vaccines must be unnecessary."
quizScenarios.explanation: One family's experience doesn't override population-level data on vaccine effectiveness and disease prevention.

cherry-picking.description: اختيار البيانات التي تدعم موقفك فقط وتجاهل البيانات التي تتعارض معه. تقديم صورة غير مكتملة لدعم استنتاج مسبق.
cherry-picking.responseStrategy: اطلب الصورة الكاملة: «هل هناك دراسات أو بيانات تتجاهلها؟ أريد رؤية جميع الأدلة، وليس فقط الأجزاء التي تدعم موقفك.»

examples.text: Our company is doing great — look at our sales in December! (Ignores declining sales for the other 11 months)
examples.explanation: Highlighting one good month while ignoring an overall downward trend is cherry picking.

examples.text: This city is very safe — here are five neighborhoods with low crime rates. (Ignores high-crime areas)
examples.explanation: Selecting only safe neighborhoods gives a misleading picture of overall city safety.

examples.text: Studies show chocolate is healthy! (Cites one small study while ignoring larger studies showing mixed results)
examples.explanation: Selecting one favorable study while ignoring the broader body of research is cherry picking.

quizScenarios.text: "Climate change isn't real — look, it snowed in April this year!"
quizScenarios.explanation: One weather event is cherry-picked to argue against a global, long-term climate trend supported by decades of data.

quizScenarios.text: "Our school's education program is excellent. Look at our top 10 students' test scores!"
quizScenarios.explanation: Showing only the best performers hides how the average or below-average students are doing.

false-equivalence.description: معاملة أشياء مختلفة بشكل كبير على أنها متساوية في المقارنة. تجاهل الاختلافات الجوهرية لخلق تكافؤ زائف.
false-equivalence.responseStrategy: أبرِز عدم التوازن: «هذان الأمران ليسا مدعومين بالتساوي. أحدهما يملك أدلة أقوى بكثير من الآخر. لنقيِّمهما بشكل متناسب.»

examples.text: Teaching both evolution and creationism in science class is fair and balanced.
examples.explanation: Evolution is supported by overwhelming scientific evidence; creationism is a religious belief. They aren't equivalent in a science context.

examples.text: Both parties are equally corrupt, so it doesn't matter who you vote for.
examples.explanation: Even if both have issues, the degree and nature of problems likely differ significantly.

examples.text: You complain about a few data breaches, but our competitor once had a server go down. We're basically the same.
examples.explanation: Data breaches and server downtime are very different in severity and implications.

quizScenarios.text: "Sure, one candidate has been convicted of fraud, but the other candidate got a parking ticket once. Both have had legal troubles, so they're equally untrustworthy."
quizScenarios.explanation: Fraud and a parking ticket are vastly different in severity. Equating them creates a false impression of equivalence.

quizScenarios.text: "Some people believe the Earth is flat and some believe it's round. Both sides deserve equal time in our documentary."
quizScenarios.explanation: The overwhelming scientific evidence supports a round Earth. Giving equal time implies equal credibility where none exists.

appeal-to-tradition.description: الادعاء بأن شيئاً ما صحيح أو أفضل لأنه كان يُفعل دائماً بهذه الطريقة. القِدم لا يجعل الممارسة صحيحة.
appeal-to-tradition.responseStrategy: اسأل لماذا التقليد مهم هنا: «المدة التي قضيناها في فعل شيء ما لا تخبرنا إن كان الأفضل. ما الأدلة التي تُظهر أن هذا لا يزال الطريق الصحيح؟»

examples.text: We've always used this filing system, so there's no reason to switch to a digital one.
examples.explanation: The age of a system doesn't mean it's still the best option.

examples.text: Marriage has always been defined this way, so it should stay the same.
examples.explanation: Historical precedent doesn't constitute a logical argument against change.

examples.text: Our family has always disciplined children this way, so it must be effective.
examples.explanation: Generational tradition doesn't prove a disciplinary method is effective or appropriate.

examples.text: Handwritten letters are more meaningful than emails because that's how people have always communicated important messages.
examples.explanation: Historical communication methods don't determine the meaningfulness of a message's content.

quizScenarios.text: "Our company has used this software for 20 years. It's worked fine all along, so there's no need to upgrade to something modern."
quizScenarios.explanation: The fact that something has been used for a long time doesn't mean it's still the best option. Technology and needs evolve.

quizScenarios.text: "People have been using this folk remedy for centuries, so it must be effective medicine."
quizScenarios.explanation: Centuries of use don't constitute clinical evidence. Many traditional remedies have been shown to be ineffective or harmful.

personal-incredulity.description: الادعاء بأن شيئاً ما غير صحيح لأنك لا تستطيع فهمه أو تخيله. عدم فهمك الشخصي لا يحدد الحقيقة.
personal-incredulity.responseStrategy: افصل الفهم عن الحقيقة: «صعوبة فهم شيء ما لا تجعله خاطئاً. لننظر إلى ما تقوله الأدلة والخبراء، لا حدسنا فقط.»

examples.text: I can't understand how evolution could produce something as complex as the human eye, so it must not be true.
examples.explanation: Personal inability to understand a process doesn't invalidate the scientific evidence supporting it.

examples.text: The universe can't possibly be expanding — I can't even imagine what it would expand into.
examples.explanation: Our inability to visualize cosmological concepts doesn't determine their truth.

examples.text: I don't see how a tiny vaccine can prevent a serious disease, so I don't trust them.
examples.explanation: Personal incomprehension of immunology doesn't override the extensive evidence for vaccine effectiveness.

quizScenarios.text: "There's no way that a computer can beat a human at chess. Computers don't think. I just can't imagine it."
quizScenarios.explanation: Personal inability to imagine something doesn't make it impossible — computers have been beating top chess players since 1997.

quizScenarios.text: "I can't wrap my head around how cryptocurrency works, so it must be a scam."
quizScenarios.explanation: Not understanding the technology doesn't determine whether it's legitimate. Complexity doesn't equal fraud.
